Photography walks, textile workshops, and studio visits with Addis artists. Bring home more than photos.
Arrive at a Hamar settlement before dawn. Watch cattle leave the boma, share black tea and sit in silence as the village wakes. Your guide — who grew up here — translates not just words but meaning.

Walk into the highland forest where Coffea arabica grows wild. Harvest cherries from trees that seed themselves. Sit with a farmer's family for a three-round coffee ceremony as rain falls on the canopy above.
Attending Timkat in Gondar changed my understanding of what a festival can be. I felt like a guest, not a tourist.

With Konijet Ethiopia, you get more than a trip — you get a real story. We help you find the true heart of this land, one friendly face and stunning view at a time.
Practical tips and vivid destinations across one extraordinary country.

Ethiopia is a land of contrasts — UNESCO sites, vibrant cultures, dramatic landscapes and unforgettable rituals. From the highlands of the north to the tribal heartlands of the south, here is everything you need to plan your journey.
Perched at 2,355m, Addis Ababa offers mild weather year-round and blends modern skyscrapers with deep tradition — coffee ceremonies, lively nightlife and the heart of African diplomacy.
National Museum — home to 'Lucy', the famous fossil
Mount Entoto — panoramic views and historic churches
Ethiopia's spiritual centre, famed for 12th-century churches carved from living stone. Pilgrims arrive in flowing white robes — most magical at sunrise during Genna and Timkat festivals.
Bete Giyorgis (St. George's) — iconic cross-shaped church
Attending Timkat in Gondar changed my understanding of what a festival can be. I felt like a guest, not a tourist.

How to experience the rock-hewn churches respectfully.

In Lalibela, whole churches were carved straight out of the living rock. These ancient sanctuaries are still active places of worship — walking through them is quiet, peaceful and feels like stepping into another world.
Often called Africa's Camelot, Gondar is home to the Fasil Ghebbi fortress, where royal stone castles still stand tall. The history here is rich and regal, capturing the imagination of every visitor.
Axum's towering stelae and ancient stone monuments mark the heart of Ethiopian Orthodox Christianity and the legendary home of the Ark of the Covenant.
Jagged peaks, dramatic escarpments and troops of gelada baboons — the Simien Mountains are nature's true masterpiece in northern Ethiopia.
One of the hottest places on Earth, Danakil features bright yellow sulfur springs, vast salt flats and active volcanoes. The land feels lunar — strange, beautiful and unforgettable.
The Omo Valley is home to tribes with rich, living traditions — body painting, lip plates, bull-jumping ceremonies — practiced daily by people who have shaped this landscape for centuries.
A maze of alleyways and over 80 mosques, Harar ends each day with the famous hyena feeding ritual outside its ancient walls.
Asheton Maryam Monastery — sweeping mountain views
Best time: January for Genna; October–March for dry weather
The legendary seat of the Queen of Sheba and the birthplace of Ethiopian Christianity. Towering stelae, royal tombs and the sacred Church of St. Mary of Zion.
A living museum of cultural diversity — Mursi lip plates, Hamar bull-jumping, Karo body painting. Always ask permission before photographing and respect tribal traditions.
Surreal salt flats, lava flows and neon sulfur springs. Erta Ale's lava lake glows red at night and Dallol's pools fluoresce yellow and green — accessible only by 4WD convoy with experienced guides.
Alpine meadows, the Harenna cloud forest and the world's rarest canid — the Ethiopian wolf — on the Sanetti Plateau.
Everything you need: arrival, visas, packing, weather and money.
Addis Ababa Bole International Airport (ADD) is Ethiopia's main gateway and one of Africa's leading aviation hubs — just 6 km from the city centre, with 15–20 minute transfers to most major hotels.
We don't do generic tours. Every itinerary is crafted to give you real encounters with Ethiopian culture, cuisine, history, and nature — not a sanitized tourist version of it.
We partner with local guides, family-run lodges, and community cooperatives. When you travel with us, your money supports Ethiopian families directly.
We are committed to low-impact tourism. We educate our travelers on environmental protection, cultural respect, and sustainable practices that protect Ethiopia's heritage for future generations.

Ethiopia is unlike anywhere else on earth. It is the only African country never colonized. It has its own calendar (13 months of sunshine), its own alphabet (Ge'ez script), its own time system, and its own coffee ceremony that has been practiced for centuries.
Other carriers: Emirates, Turkish, Qatar, Lufthansa, FlyDubai, EgyptAir
Domestic flights cover Lalibela, Gondar, Axum, Bahir Dar, Arba Minch, Jinka, Mekele
On arrival, your Konijet guide will meet you at arrivals with a name sign

Most international travellers can obtain an Ethiopian visa via e-Visa (recommended) at evisa.gov.et, or visa-on-arrival at Bole International Airport. Your passport must be valid for at least six months from entry.
Most days involve 8–9 hours on the go — sights, lunch breaks, photo stops and drive time. A few days extend to 10+ hours. Days generally start between 7:30–8:30 and finish around 16:00–17:00.
We use a private bus charter for the northern loop (Addis to Lalibela), domestic flights as stated in the itinerary, and air-conditioned 4WD Land Cruisers for off-road sections.
Expect sunshine most days. The north stays comfortably warm; the south can reach 30°C+. Nights in the highlands can be cold — bring a jacket and light thermals.
The Danakil Depression is one of the hottest places on Earth. Trips run October–February, but daytime temperatures can still reach 40°C.
Dress is conservative. Comfortable walking shoes with ankle support are strongly recommended, plus a hat and sunscreen.

Konijet Ethiopia was founded with one purpose: to show the world what Ethiopians already know — that this ancient land is one of the most breathtaking, culturally rich, and spiritually profound destinations on Earth.

The name "Konijet" (ቆጂት) means beautiful in Amharic. It is what we see every time we look at our country — beautiful mountains, beautiful people, beautiful history. We wanted to create a company worthy of that name.

We started as a small team of passionate Ethiopian guides and travel professionals who were tired of seeing Ethiopia overlooked on the world tourism map. Ethiopia is the cradle of humankind, the birthplace of coffee, home to one of the world's oldest Christian civilizations, and a land of extraordinary biodiversity. Yet it remains one of Africa's best-kept secrets. We are here to change that.

Today, Konijet Ethiopia offers curated tour packages spanning the entire country — from the rock-hewn churches of Lalibela to the volcanic landscapes of the Danakil Depression, from the ancient obelisks of Axum to the tribal cultures of the Omo Valley, and from the wildlife of the Simien Mountains to the shores of Lake Tana.

To create transformative travel experiences that connect visitors to the authentic heart of Ethiopia, while supporting local communities and preserving the natural and cultural heritage of our nation.
We don't do generic tours. Every itinerary is crafted to give you real encounters with Ethiopian culture, cuisine, history, and nature — not a sanitized tourist version of it.
We partner with local guides, family-run lodges, and community cooperatives. When you travel with us, your money supports Ethiopian families directly.
We are committed to low-impact tourism. We educate our travelers on environmental protection, cultural respect, and sustainable practices that protect Ethiopia's heritage for future generations.

Ethiopia is unlike anywhere else on earth. It is the only African country never colonized. It has its own calendar (13 months of sunshine), its own alphabet (Ge'ez script), its own time system, and its own coffee ceremony that has been practiced for centuries.
When you visit Ethiopia, you are not just taking a holiday. You are stepping into human history.

Konijet Ethiopia Tourism is committed to protecting your personal information and your right to privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit konjetethiopia.com and use our services.
By accessing or using konjetethiopia.com and our services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service and all applicable laws and regulations. If you do not agree, please do not use this site.
Cooking with families. Trekking with shepherds. Listening with elders. These are the moments that don't appear in guidebooks — and the only ones you'll still remember in twenty years.

Ethiopia is one of the oldest civilisations on Earth. It deserves more than a highlight reel. We build itineraries around real connection — with communities, landscapes and histories that will reconfigure how you see the world.
Sleep in family homes, join daily routines, and share meals made from scratch. Not a hotel. Not a tour group. A life.
Trace Ethiopia's wild coffee from forest to cup — harvest, wash, roast and brew alongside the farmers who grow it.
Trek shepherd trails through the Simien escarpment, track wolves across the Bale plateau, and sleep under Africa's clearest skies.
Hands-on injera making, spice market tours and home-kitchen dinners. Ethiopian cuisine as it is actually eaten.
Join Timkat processions, Hamar bull-jumping and Meskel bonfires — not as a spectator, but as a welcomed guest.
Photography walks, textile workshops, and studio visits with Addis artists. Bring home more than photos.
Arrive at a Hamar settlement before dawn. Watch cattle leave the boma, share black tea and sit in silence as the village wakes. Your guide — who grew up here — translates not just words but meaning.

Walk into the highland forest where Coffea arabica grows wild. Harvest cherries from trees that seed themselves. Sit with a farmer's family for a three-round coffee ceremony as rain falls on the canopy above.

Descend to 120m below sea level and watch sulphur springs bubble in technicolour. Sleep beside Afar salt miners who have worked this landscape for a thousand years. The most extreme landscape on Earth — with the warmest hosts.

Stand in the carved rock courtyard at 2 am on Ethiopian Christmas Eve. A priest chants in Ge'ez from memory. Thousands of white-robed pilgrims hold candles. No other experience on earth is quite like this.
The coffee forest walk in Kaffa was the single best day of my entire year of travelling. Konijet's guide knew every family on the path.
I've done safaris in Kenya and Tanzania. The Omo Valley with Konijet is a completely different category — quieter, more personal, more real.
Attending Timkat in Gondar changed my understanding of what a festival can be. I felt like a guest, not a tourist.

Ancient faith, vibrant tribes and the rhythm of 80+ languages.

Ethiopia is a nation of incredible destinations — high mountains, deep valleys and a very long, layered history. Each place tells a different story, and together they form one of Africa's most distinctive cultural journeys.

In Lalibela, whole churches were carved straight out of the living rock. These ancient sanctuaries are still active places of worship — walking through them is quiet, peaceful and feels like stepping into another world.
Often called Africa's Camelot, Gondar is home to the Fasil Ghebbi fortress, where royal stone castles still stand tall. The history here is rich and regal, capturing the imagination of every visitor.
Axum's towering stelae and ancient stone monuments mark the heart of Ethiopian Orthodox Christianity and the legendary home of the Ark of the Covenant.
Jagged peaks, dramatic escarpments and troops of gelada baboons — the Simien Mountains are nature's true masterpiece in northern Ethiopia.
One of the hottest places on Earth, Danakil features bright yellow sulfur springs, vast salt flats and active volcanoes. The land feels lunar — strange, beautiful and unforgettable.
The Omo Valley is home to tribes with rich, living traditions — body painting, lip plates, bull-jumping ceremonies — practiced daily by people who have shaped this landscape for centuries.
A maze of alleyways and over 80 mosques, Harar ends each day with the famous hyena feeding ritual outside its ancient walls.
With Konijet Ethiopia, you get more than a trip — you get a real story. We help you find the true heart of this land, one friendly face and stunning view at a time.

Local currency is the Ethiopian Birr (ETB). Credit cards are accepted in major hotels but limited elsewhere. ATMs are available in major towns. Bring USD, EUR or GBP cash in high-denomination notes (post-1999, undamaged).

The national dish is injera — a circular fermented teff pancake — served with spiced meats, vegetables and sauces seasoned with berbere. Vegetarians should try 'fasting food', a colourful spread of salads and vegetable stews.

Konijet Ethiopia carefully selects high-quality hotels, resorts and eco-lodges across the country. In Addis we partner with Sheraton, Hyatt Regency, Ethiopian Skylight, Hilton, Radisson Blu and Golden Tulip. In remote regions, accommodation is simpler but always clean and welcoming.
